title: Sentinel: Producer Inbound Pipeline (verify proof, verify VP/VC, trust checks, policy eval, forward to backend)
description: Implement the complete producer-side inbound request verification pipeline. Every incoming request from a consumer sentinel must pass through an 8-stage verification pipeline before being forwarded to the backend service. The pipeline enforces: request size/timeout limits, ProofClaims signature and binding verification, VP/VC cryptographic verification, issuer trust checks (via TrustLayerClient), revocation freshness (via RevocationManager), policy evaluation (ABAC), and finally header scrubbing before forwarding. On permit, forward. On deny, return structured 401/403 with only an error code and request_id — never leak internal claim details.
objectives: Implement catch-all route /{full_path:path} for all HTTP methods on producer sentinel
8-stage pipeline: size/timeout → extract headers → verify proof → verify VP/VC → trust checks → revocation → policy → forward
On permit: scrub SentinelProof and SentinelVP headers before forwarding to backend
On deny: return 401/403 with {error: <code>, request_id: <trace_id>} only
Log every decision with: decision, trace_id, jti_hash (SHA-256 truncated to 16 chars), consumer_did_hash (SHA-256 truncated to 16 chars), service_id, latency_ms

steps.title: Register the catch-all inbound route
steps.detail: In `sentinel/producer/routes/inbound.py`: `@router.api_route('/{full_path:path}', methods=['GET','POST','PUT','PATCH','DELETE','OPTIONS','HEAD'])`. Handler receives `request: Request` and `full_path: str`. Instantiate `InboundPipeline` (injected via dependency) and call `await pipeline.process(request, full_path)`. Return the pipeline result as a `Response` object (either forwarded backend response or deny response).

steps.title: Stage 1: Request size and timeout enforcement
steps.detail: Read `Content-Length` header. If present and > 2MB: immediately return 413 `{error: 'REQUEST_TOO_LARGE'}` without reading body. If absent (chunked), read body up to 2MB + 1 byte; if exceeded, return 413. Set a stage timeout: if any stage takes > `PIPELINE_STAGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` (default 10s), cancel and return 504 `{error: 'PIPELINE_TIMEOUT'}`. Use `asyncio.wait_for` around the entire pipeline (excluding network I/O to backend).

steps.title: Stage 2: Extract security headers
steps.detail: Extract: `authorization = request.headers.get('Authorization', '')`. Check prefix `'SentinelProof '`. If missing or wrong prefix: return 401 `{error: 'MISSING_PROOF'}`. Extract `vp_jwt = request.headers.get('SentinelVP', '')`. If missing: return 401 `{error: 'MISSING_VP'}`. Extract `correlation_id = request.headers.get('X-Correlation-ID', generate_uuid())`. Body bytes are already read in Stage 1 — pass them through. Do not at this stage validate the JWT structure — that happens in Stage 3.

steps.title: Stage 3: Verify ProofClaims (signature, temporal, binding)
steps.detail: Call `await ProofVerifier.verify(proof_jwt, expected_aud=self.service_did, expected_env=self.env, resolver=resolver, trust_client=trust_client, replay_cache=replay_cache)`. This performs: (a) JWT decode + sig verify, (b) exp/iat temporal check with max_clock_skew from PolicyParams, (c) aud and env binding, (d) body_hash recomputation over body bytes from Stage 1, (e) query_hash recomputation from request URL, (f) jti replay cache check-and-insert. On any failure, return 401 with the specific error code from `ProofErrorCode`. On success, receive `VerificationContext(consumer_did, jti, proof_claims)`.

steps.title: Stage 4: Verify VP and VCs
steps.detail: Call `await verify_vp(vp_jwt, expected_aud=self.service_did, expected_nonce=proof.jti, expected_env=self.env, resolver=resolver, trusted_issuer_dids=trusted_dids_set)`. The `trusted_dids_set` is fetched from TrustLayerClient's issuer cache (all active issuers). Verify: (a) VP JWS signature, (b) aud == service_did, (c) nonce == proof.jti (VP bound to this exact request), (d) Each VC in VP: signature valid, not expired, issuer trusted. On failure: return 401 `{error: 'VP_INVALID'}` or `{error: 'VP_VC_INVALID'}` based on failure source. On success, receive `VerifiedPresentation` with list of `VerifiedCredential`.

steps.title: Stage 5: Issuer trust checks via TrustLayerClient
steps.detail: For each VC in the verified presentation: call `await trust_client.is_issuer_trusted(vc.issuer_did, schema_id=vc.credential_type[0])`. If any VC has an untrusted issuer: return 401 `{error: 'ISSUER_NOT_TRUSTED'}`. If TrustLayerClient raises TrustLayerUnavailable: apply local fail-closed — return 503 `{error: 'TRUST_LAYER_UNAVAILABLE', request_id}`. This is a transient error; consumer should retry. Do NOT permit the request through if trust cannot be verified.

steps.title: Stage 6: Revocation freshness check
steps.detail: For each VC with a `credentialStatus` field: call `await revocation_manager.check(vc.jti, status_list_id, status_list_index)` (TASK-046). RevocationManager enforces Δ-bounded freshness — if status data is stale beyond Δ, it returns a staleness policy decision. If any VC is revoked: return 401 `{error: 'VC_REVOKED'}`. If RevocationManager returns staleness violation and policy is fail-closed: return 503 `{error: 'REVOCATION_STATUS_STALE'}`. If policy is degrade: continue but set `stale_revocation=True` flag in context for audit log.

steps.title: Stage 7: Policy evaluation (ABAC)
steps.detail: Call `policy_engine.evaluate(resource=full_path, method=request.method, env=self.env, consumer_did=consumer_did, vcs=verified_vcs)` (TASK-047). Returns `PolicyDecision(permit=bool, rule_id=str, missing_scopes=list, reason=str)`. On Deny: log the `rule_id` and `missing_scopes` internally (NOT in response). Return 403 `{error: 'POLICY_DENY', request_id}`. On Permit: proceed to Stage 8.

steps.title: Stage 8: Forward to backend and return response
steps.detail: Construct forwarded request: copy all headers from original, REMOVE `Authorization` (SentinelProof), `SentinelVP`, and `X-Internal-*` headers. Add `X-Sentinel-Consumer-DID-Hash` (first 16 chars of SHA-256 of consumer_did — not the full DID), `X-Sentinel-Request-ID` (correlation_id), `X-Sentinel-Env` (env). Forward to `BACKEND_URL + '/' + full_path` using shared HTTPX client with timeout `BACKEND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` (default 30s). Stream response body back. On backend error (5xx): return 502 to consumer. On backend timeout: return 504.

steps.title: Structured logging and metrics
steps.detail: After every request (permit or deny), emit a structured log: `{ts, decision: permit|deny, error_code, trace_id, jti_hash: sha256(jti)[:16], consumer_did_hash: sha256(consumer_did)[:16], service_id, path, method, latency_ms, stage_when_denied, stale_revocation}`. Never log full jti, full consumer_did, or VC content. Prometheus: `inbound_requests_total{decision, error_code}` Counter, `pipeline_stage_duration_seconds{stage}` Histogram, `inbound_request_size_bytes` Histogram. OTel span `sentinel.inbound_pipeline` with attributes matching log fields.

files.sentinel/producer/pipeline_context.py: PipelineContext dataclass threading state through stages

security_constraints: SentinelProof and SentinelVP headers MUST be removed before forwarding to backend — backend must never see raw JWT material
Deny responses MUST NOT include claim values, VC content, or consumer DID in response body
body_hash MUST be recomputed over the exact bytes received — no normalization before hashing
Replay cache check (jti) MUST complete before VP verification to fail-fast on replays
Stage timeouts prevent slow-consumer DoS — backend call timeout is separate from pipeline timeout
Consumer DID hash in forwarded header uses only first 16 chars to prevent full DID reconstruction
acceptance_criteria: All 8 pipeline stages execute in correct order with correct error codes on failure
Backend receives request without SentinelProof/SentinelVP headers
Replay of same proof (jti) is rejected on second attempt
Policy deny returns 403 with no VC claim details in response
Structured decision log emitted for every request (permit and deny)
Pipeline completes valid requests in under 20ms (excluding backend I/O)
